      <title><![CDATA[RBI tells parliamentary panel cryptocurrencies should not be legalised in India, cites risks to economy]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/economy/rbi-opposes-cryptocurrency-legalisation-india-parliamentary-panel-19937699.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[The Reserve Bank of India has reiterated its opposition to legalising cryptocurrencies, telling a parliamentary panel that virtual digital assets pose significant risks to an emerging economy and could facilitate illicit activities such as terror financing and narcotics smuggling.       <description><![CDATA[Nine years after GST transformed India's indirect tax system, the focus has shifted from implementation to improving how the regime functions. While GST 2.0 simplified tax rates, experts say the next stage of reforms should prioritise reducing litigation, fixing the inverted duty structure, expanding GST to more sectors and creating a long-term roadmap to make the tax system simpler, more predictable and business-friendly.       <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/economy/gst-nine-years-explained-key-reforms-challenges-and-whats-next-19936581.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[The Goods and Services Tax has reshaped India's indirect tax landscape by expanding the taxpayer base, improving compliance and driving record revenue collections over the past nine years. Here's a look at how GST has evolved since 2017, the reforms introduced along the way and the key policy priorities likely to shape its next phase.]]></description>

    <item>
      <title><![CDATA[What's keeping Silicon Valley's AI leaders up at night]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/technology/silicon-valley-ceos-biggest-ai-concerns-explained-19935543.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[The AI boom has minted trillion-dollar companies and triggered an unprecedented investment frenzy, but Silicon Valley's biggest leaders are increasingly focused on the risks that come with it. From soaring power consumption and rising AI infrastructure costs to enterprise adoption, data security, workforce disruption and fears of an eventual shakeout, top CEOs tell CNBC-TV18 that the next phase of AI will be defined less by hype and more by execution. Their insights reveal why scaling artificial intelligence sustainably may prove far harder than building it.]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Tue, 30 Jun 2026 18:03:36 +0530]]></pubDate>
      <dc:creator>Swapnil Deshpande</dc:creator>
      <guid><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/technology/silicon-valley-ceos-biggest-ai-concerns-explained-19935543.htm]]></guid>

    <item>
      <title><![CDATA[Explained: AI's impact on senior level roles]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/technology/ai-impact-on-ceos-c-suite-leadership-workplace-transformation-explained-19934485.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[Artificial intelligence is no longer just transforming entry-level jobs—it is beginning to reshape the boardroom itself. As CEOs increasingly rely on AI for decision-making, companies are being forced to rethink how work is organised, how managers create value, and how governance keeps pace with rapid technological adoption. Research from IBM, Gartner and McKinsey suggests that the competitive advantage will come not from buying the most powerful AI models, but from redesigning workflows and operating models around them. While AI is unlikely to replace senior executives anytime soon, it is changing what leadership is expected to deliver, making CEOs, CFOs and CIOs more accountable for productivity, governance, cost control and enterprise-wide AI strategy.]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Mon, 29 Jun 2026 23:16:25 +0530]]></pubDate>
      <dc:creator>Swapnil Deshpande</dc:creator>
      <guid><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/technology/ai-impact-on-ceos-c-suite-leadership-workplace-transformation-explained-19934485.htm]]></guid>

    </item>
